scenarios.title: Screen cracked from accidental drop — first ADH claim
scenarios.description: Customer dropped their iPhone 14 Pro on concrete, resulting in a visibly cracked display. This is the first accidental damage claim under the plan.

service_fee.notes: Standard ADH service fee applies for screen damage

policy_references.snippet: If during the Plan Term you submit a valid claim notifying Apple that the Covered iPhone has failed due to accidental damage from handling ("ADH"), Apple will...repair or exchange the Covered iPhone...subject to the service fee described below.
You will pay a $49 (U.S.) dollar service fee plus applicable tax for each ADH claim.

scenarios.reasoning: The policy explicitly covers accidental damage from handling such as drops, subject to a $49 service fee per claim and a two-claim limit per plan term (section 3.2). This is the first ADH claim for this device, so coverage applies.

scenarios.title: Battery capacity below 50% after 20 months
scenarios.description: After 20 months of use, the iPhone 13 battery health shows 47% of original capacity. No accidental or intentional damage is reported.

service_fee.notes: No fee for battery depletion service

policy_references.snippet: If...the capacity of the Covered iPhone’s battery to hold an electrical charge has depleted fifty (50%) percent or more from its original specifications, Apple will...repair...or...exchange the Covered iPhone.

scenarios.reasoning: Battery capacity depletion to 50% or below is specifically covered under section 3.1, with no service fee and no impact on accidental damage claim limits.
